title: gattenhoffia pluvialis
side_effects: Gattenhoffia pluvialis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to liver damage and kidney failure. In severe cases, improper use of Gattenhoffia pluvialis can result in seizures, coma, and even death. Additionally, it can also cause skin irritation, rashes, and allergic reactions in some individuals.
